## Description

Brief summary of changes in this PR. What problem does it solve? What does it implement?

Fixes #(issue number, e.g., #42)

## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Performance improvement
- [ ] Code refactoring

## Changes Made

Describe the specific changes:

- Changed X to Y because reason
- Added new function `foo()` to handle scenario
- Updated CSS for responsive design
- Refactored data model to improve performance

## Testing

How did you test this change?

### Test Cases

- [ ] Test case 1: Scenario and expected result
- [ ] Test case 2: Scenario and expected result
- [ ] Tested on browser: Chrome / Safari / Firefox / Edge / Mobile
- [ ] Tested offline scenario (if applicable)
- [ ] Tested permission gates (if role-based)

### Manual Testing

Steps to test this PR:

1. Check out branch
2. Open app → [page/feature]
3. [Action] → [verify result]

## Screenshots / Videos

If UI changes, include:

- Before/after screenshots
- Videos of flow (if complex interaction)
- Mobile view (if responsive changes)

## Performance Impact

- [ ] No performance change
- [ ] Performance improvement (describe)
- [ ] Performance regression (describe mitigation)

Does this change require data migration?

- [ ] No data changes
- [ ] Data migration included in PR
- [ ] Existing data still works
- [ ] Backup/recovery tested

## Breaking Changes

- [ ] No breaking changes
- [ ] Breaking change (describe): Changes to [API/data/behavior] that will affect [users/integrations]

## Checklist

- [ ] I have self-reviewed my code
- [ ] I have commented complex sections
- [ ] I have updated documentation (README, docs, etc)
- [ ] I have added tests (if applicable)
- [ ] All tests pass locally
- [ ] My changes generate no new console errors/warnings
- [ ] I have removed debug code
- [ ] I have checked for console.log statements left behind
